_meta.description: EDCM Operator bone inventory: punctuation and connector rules.
_meta.attribution: Claude generated; context, prompt Erin Spencer.
_meta.bone_membership_test: Same as words/affixes: does this punctuation mark create, redirect, or resolve a constraint relationship? Boundary markers that merely segment do not emit tokens.
notes: Most rules here are already frozen in canon (edcmbone_v1 §2.5-§2.8).
This file consolidates and extends with the same principles.
Punctuation that only segments text (periods, commas) is boundary-only: 0 tokens.
Punctuation that creates constraint topology (?, !, ;, :, ...) emits tokens.
hmmm: Exclamation mark as P — it asserts, but is it structural enough? Included with flag.
Ellipsis as Q — it creates a gap, but the gap may be rhetorical not interrogative. Included.
Colon and semicolon as K — they link clauses, but differently than conjunctions. Included.
Parentheses — structural aside. Currently boundary-only. May deserve S token.
Quotation marks — attribution marker. Currently boundary-only. May deserve S token for 'He Said/She Said' parsing.

punctuation.notes: Interrogative marker. Opens a constraint gap.

Assertion/exclamation marker. Asserts or intensifies a constraint position. Creates polarity pressure. NOT a degree modifier — it changes the constraint topology of the sentence from statement to assertion.

punctuation.rule: Exactly 1 Q token per ellipsis (regardless of dot count).
punctuation.notes: Trailing gap / unfinished constraint. Creates an open space. Normalize 2+ dots to single ellipsis unit.
Clause linkage without conjunction. Creates a relational connection between independent clauses.
Introduces elaboration, list, or explanation. Links a setup to its payload.

punctuation.rule: Boundary marker only. Emits 0 operator tokens. Preserved losslessly.
punctuation.notes: Em dash. Interruption, aside, or break. Segments but does not create constraint pressure.

punctuation.rule: Exactly 1 K token per en dash.
punctuation.notes: En dash. Range or connection (1990–2000, London–Paris). Relational linkage.

punctuation.rule: Exactly 1 K token per HYPHENATED COMPOUND WORD (not per hyphen). state-of-the-art → 1 K. Internal free bones processed normally.
punctuation.notes: Hyphen in compound words. Creates compound linkage.

punctuation.rule: Ignored for operator counting in v1. Preserved losslessly.
punctuation.notes: Slash. May later map to K (alternative/relation). Deferred decision.

punctuation.rule: Boundary marker only. Emits 0 operator tokens.
punctuation.notes: Period. Sentence boundary. Segments but does not create constraint pressure.

punctuation.rule: Boundary marker only. Emits 0 operator tokens.
punctuation.notes: Comma. Clause/phrase separator. Segments but does not create constraint pressure.

punctuation.rule: Boundary markers only. Emit 0 operator tokens. Preserved losslessly.
punctuation.notes: Parentheses. Structural aside. Does not create constraint pressure in v1.

punctuation.rule: Boundary markers only. Emit 0 operator tokens.
punctuation.notes: Quotation marks. Attribution boundary. Relevant to He Said/She Said parsing but not to operator counting.
